debt_share_of_RD.source: Gao (NUS), citing Brown, Fazzari & Petersen (2009): median short- and long-term debt-to-assets = 0.005 each for high-tech firms, 2000s; 'low reliance on debt financing'
debt_share_of_RD.corroboration: Hall & Lerner (2009): debt is a disfavored source of R&D finance; R&D intensity and leverage negatively correlated (Friend-Lang 1988; Hall 1992; Bhagat-Welch 1995)

rd_composition.source: NSF NCSES 2023 (nsf25353, nsf26314): $722B business R&D, ~87-89% company own-funds; total US R&D ~$937B, business 75% / federal 18%
rd_composition.dominant_external_channel: external equity, not debt (Brown & Petersen 2009/2011: 'neither debt finance nor cash flow' funds the rise in R&D)

rd_usercost_elasticity.source: CRS R48848: recent R&D user-cost elasticity -2.0 to -4.0; older -1.0

debt_cost_increase_from_full_reserve.note: Modelled as an intermediation-cost premium on the debt slice, not a ban. Bounded 20-50% adversarially. CS Paper 6 frames full reserve as raising intermediation cost, offsetting credit-elasticity loss to near-full coverage.
